claim: Long-running agents require three non-negotiable features: durable state checkpoints (not JSON chat logs), event-driven dormancy (true sleep, no active polling), and separated evaluation (planner, generator, evaluator)
actionable: Build agent workflows with durable state in database enums, webhook-triggered resumption instead of polling loops, and separate evaluator agents that do not grade their own work
source_title: Build long-running agents with Google's Agentic Stack | The Agent Factory

claim: Agents should not grade their own work; agents systematically overrate their own output and self-correction creates mediocre quality; use separate evaluator agents
actionable: Implement a three-agent pattern: planner (decides what to do), generator (executes), evaluator (tests real output); do not let the generator evaluate itself
source_title: Build long-running agents with Google's Agentic Stack | The Agent Factory

claim: Long-running agents operating for hours/days need deterministic state management where workflow steps are sequenced in state machine order and cannot be skipped or reordered by the agent
actionable: Encode workflow sequences as state enums in database; each agent step advances the state machine to the next valid state, preventing deviation from intended process flow
source_title: Build long-running agents with Google's Agentic Stack | The Agent Factory

claim: Event-driven dormancy (webhooks, schedules, human approvals triggering agent resume) is cheaper and more reliable than active polling loops burning compute while waiting
actionable: Use external event listeners (webhooks from shipping providers, calendar events, human approval APIs) to resume agents rather than looping on status checks
source_title: Build long-running agents with Google's Agentic Stack | The Agent Factory

claim: Multi-agent delegation allows the main orchestrator to stay focused while specialized sub-agents (IT provisioning, hardware tracking) handle domain-specific work without context bloat
actionable: When agents delegate work to sub-agents, keep the main agent's context clean by having sub-agents return only summaries, not full execution logs
source_title: Build long-running agents with Google's Agentic Stack | The Agent Factory

claim: Using coding agents for truly long-running work requires accepting that code generation is inherently iterative; agents must self-correct across multiple turns until the goal is met
actionable: Set agents a single goal/spec and let them iterate autonomously across multiple turns, correcting errors as they encounter them, rather than expecting first-pass completion
source_title: Build long-running agents with Google's Agentic Stack | The Agent Factory

claim: Cognitive debt occurs when engineers defer all reasoning to agents and lose understanding of how the system works; this leads to cognitive surrender where engineers cannot debug production issues
actionable: Maintain human understanding of your codebase; use agents to accelerate 70-80% of work but ensure the final 20-30% involves human judgment, comprehension, and verification
source_title: Build long-running agents with Google's Agentic Stack | The Agent Factory

claim: Agent-generated code requires explicit verification strategies: extensive tests, visual regression testing, or clear definition of quality standards so agents stay within bounds
actionable: Define your quality bar clearly (test coverage, performance benchmarks, code review criteria); have agents verify their output against these standards before shipping
source_title: Build long-running agents with Google's Agentic Stack | The Agent Factory

claim: Agentic Engine Optimization (AEO) makes documentation consumable by AI agents; for developer products, good AEO (structured skill definitions, tool documentation) dramatically improves agent effectiveness
actionable: If building a developer product, invest in making your docs, skill definitions, and API specs easily consumable by agents; this is as important as SEO was for search
source_title: Build long-running agents with Google's Agentic Stack | The Agent Factory
